revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 佐麻久嶺神社 |画像 = [[File:Samakumine Jinja Haiden.jpg|270px|]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[福島県]][[いわき市]]平中山宮下19 | 緯度度 = 37 |緯度分 = 2 |緯度秒 = 9 | 経度度 = 140 |経度分 = 54 |経度秒 = 46 |ラベル位置 = left |祭神 = [[五十猛命]] |神体 = |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）
旧[[郷社]] |創建 = [[慶雲]]元年（[[704年]]） |本殿 = [[流造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[5月4日]] |神事 = |地図 = Japan Fukushima }} [[File:Samakumine Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''佐麻久嶺神社'''（さまくみねじんじゃ）は、[[福島県]][[いわき市]]にある[[神社]]。[[延喜式神名帳|式内小社]]で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は郷社。 == 歴史 == 社伝によると、[[慶雲]]元年（[[704年]]）の創建とされる。 [[延喜式神名帳]]では、[[陸奥国]][[石城郡|磐城郡]]七座の一座として小社に列した。[[江戸時代]]には[[磐城平藩]]より崇敬を受け、[[天和]]2年（1682年）に落雷で焼失した際は翌年に藩主[[内藤義概|内藤義泰]]により社殿が再建され、以後も歴代藩主より改修がなされた。 [[明治]]9年郷社に列した。 == 境内 == File:Samakumine Jinja Honden.jpg|本殿 File:Samakumine Jinja Kaguraden.jpg|神楽殿 == 関連項目 == * [[陸奥国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [http://xn--3iq3y595abz0ateam90u.jp/ 佐麻久嶺神社]（公式サイト） {{デフォルトソート:さまくみねしんしや}} [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:宮城県の旧郷社]] [[Category:いわき市の建築物]] [[Category:いわき市の歴史]] [[Category:磐城平藩]]

{{神社 |名称 = 佐麻久嶺神社 |画像 = [[File:Samakumine Jinja Haiden.jpg|270px|]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "{{ill|Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei}}" to "{{ill|Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}}[[Category:Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)]]"

Bot: add interwikis from Wikidata
revision.text:

{{nihongo|'''Samakumine Shrine'''|佐麻久嶺神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Mutsu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Samakumine Shrine'''|佐麻久嶺神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Mutsu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Samakumine Shrine'''|佐麻久嶺神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Mutsu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Samakumine Shrine'''|佐麻久嶺神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Mutsu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Samakumine Shrine'''|佐麻久嶺神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Mutsu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Samakumine Shrine'''|佐麻久嶺神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Mutsu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Samakumine Shrine'''|佐麻久嶺神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Mutsu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Samakumine Shrine'''|佐麻久嶺神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Mutsu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Samakumine Shrine'''|佐麻久嶺神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Mutsu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Samakumine Shrine'''|佐麻久嶺神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Mutsu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Samakumine Shrine'''|佐麻久嶺神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Mutsu Province]].

{{Infobox religious building | name = Samakumine Shrine | native_name = 佐麻久嶺神社 | image = Samakumine Jinja Haiden.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Samakumine Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|37.035703|140.912733}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }}
{{nihongo|'''Samakumine Shrine'''|佐麻久嶺神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Mutsu Province]].

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 佐麻久嶺神社 |画像 = [[File:Samakumine Jinja Haiden.jpg|270px|]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[福島県]][[いわき市]][[平中山]]字宮下81 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[五十猛命]] |神体 = |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）
旧[[郷社]] |創建 = [[慶雲]]元年（[[704年]]） |本殿 = [[流造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[5月4日]] |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=佐麻久嶺
神社|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Samakumine Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''佐麻久嶺神社'''（さまくみねじんじゃ）は、[[福島県]][[いわき市]][[平中山]]にある[[神社]]。[[延喜式神名帳|式内小社]]で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は郷社。 == 歴史 == 社伝によると、[[慶雲]]元年（[[704年]]）の創建とされる。 [[延喜式神名帳]]では、[[陸奥国]][[石城郡|磐城郡]]七座の一座として小社に列した。[[江戸時代]]には[[磐城平藩]]より崇敬を受け、[[天和 (日本)|天和]]2年（[[1682年]]）に落雷で焼失した際は翌年に藩主[[内藤義概|内藤義泰]]により社殿が再建され、以後も歴代藩主より改修がなされた。 [[明治]]9年（[[1876年]]）郷社に列した。 == 境内 == File:Samakumine Jinja Honden.jpg|本殿 File:Samakumine Jinja Kaguraden.jpg|神楽殿 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[陸奥国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [http://xn--3iq3y595abz0ateam90u.jp/ 佐麻久嶺神社]（公式サイト） {{神道 横}} {{デフォルトソート:さまくみねしんしや}} [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:福島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:いわき市の建築物]] [[Category:いわき市の歴史]] [[Category:磐城平藩]]

[[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] {{translated page|ja|佐麻久嶺神社|version=107318972|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]] == Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称 = 佐麻久嶺神社 |画像 = [[File:Samakumine Jinja Haiden.jpg|270px|]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[福島県]][[いわき市]][[平中山]]字宮下81 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[五十猛命]] |神体 = |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）
旧[[郷社]] |創建 = [[慶雲]]元年（[[704年]]） |本殿 = [[流造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[5月4日]] |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=佐麻久嶺
神社|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Samakumine Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''佐麻久嶺神社'''（さまくみねじんじゃ）は、[[福島県]][[いわき市]][[平中山]]にある[[神社]]。[[延喜式神名帳|式内小社]]で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は郷社。 == 歴史 == 社伝によると、[[慶雲]]元年（[[704年]]）の創建とされる。 [[延喜式神名帳]]では、[[陸奥国]][[石城郡|磐城郡]]七座の一座として小社に列した。[[江戸時代]]には[[磐城平藩]]より崇敬を受け、[[天和 (日本)|天和]]2年（[[1682年]]）に落雷で焼失した際は翌年に藩主[[内藤義概|内藤義泰]]により社殿が再建され、以後も歴代藩主より改修がなされた。 [[明治]]9年（[[1876年]]）郷社に列した。 == 境内 == File:Samakumine Jinja Honden.jpg|本殿 File:Samakumine Jinja Kaguraden.jpg|神楽殿 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[陸奥国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [http://xn--3iq3y595abz0ateam90u.jp/ 佐麻久嶺神社]（公式サイト） {{神道 横}} [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:福島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:いわき市の建築物]] [[Category:いわき市の歴史]] [[Category:磐城平藩]] ==End Japanese==

[[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] {{translated page|ja|佐麻久嶺神社|version=107318972|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]] == Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称 = 佐麻久嶺神社 |画像 = [[File:Samakumine Jinja Haiden.jpg|270px|]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[福島県]][[いわき市]][[平中山]]字宮下81 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[五十猛命]] |神体 = |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）
旧[[郷社]] |創建 = [[慶雲]]元年（[[704年]]） |本殿 = [[流造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[5月4日]] |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=佐麻久嶺
神社|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Samakumine Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''佐麻久嶺神社'''（さまくみねじんじゃ）は、[[福島県]][[いわき市]][[平中山]]にある[[神社]]。[[延喜式神名帳|式内小社]]で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は郷社。 == 歴史 == 社伝によると、[[慶雲]]元年（[[704年]]）の創建とされる。 [[延喜式神名帳]]では、[[陸奥国]][[石城郡|磐城郡]]七座の一座として小社に列した。[[江戸時代]]には[[磐城平藩]]より崇敬を受け、[[天和 (日本)|天和]]2年（[[1682年]]）に落雷で焼失した際は翌年に藩主[[内藤義概|内藤義泰]]により社殿が再建され、以後も歴代藩主より改修がなされた。 [[明治]]9年（[[1876年]]）郷社に列した。 == 境内 == File:Samakumine Jinja Honden.jpg|本殿 File:Samakumine Jinja Kaguraden.jpg|神楽殿 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[陸奥国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [http://xn--3iq3y595abz0ateam90u.jp/ 佐麻久嶺神社]（公式サイト） {{神道 横}} ==End Japanese== [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:福島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:いわき市の建築物]] [[Category:いわき市の歴史]] [[Category:磐城平藩]]

{{nihongo|'''Samakumine Shrine'''|佐麻久嶺神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Mutsu Province]].

神社|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Samakumine Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''佐麻久嶺神社'''（さまくみねじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|福島県|Q71707}}{{jalink|いわき市|Q274384}}{{jalink|平中山|Q128846768}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内小社}}で、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は郷社。 === 歴史 === 社伝によると、{{jalink|慶雲|Q428753}}元年（{{jalink|704年|Q23503}}）の創建とされる。 {{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}では、{{jalink|陸奥国|Q907389}}{{jalink|石城郡|Q11585428|磐城郡}}七座の一座として小社に列した。{{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}には{{jalink|磐城平藩|Q843782}}より崇敬を受け、{{jalink|天和 (日本)|Q1145289|天和}}2年（{{jalink|1682年|Q7648}}）に落雷で焼失した際は翌年に藩主{{jalink|内藤義概|Q8514975|内藤義泰}}により社殿が再建され、以後も歴代藩主より改修がなされた。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}9年（{{jalink|1876年|Q7766}}）郷社に列した。 === 境内 === File:Samakumine Jinja Honden.jpg|本殿 File:Samakumine Jinja Kaguraden.jpg|神楽殿 === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} === 関連項目 === * {{jalink|陸奥国の式内社一覧|Q11658590}} === 外部リンク === * [http://xn--3iq3y595abz0ateam90u.jp/ 佐麻久嶺神社]（公式サイト） {{神道 横}} {{デフォルトソート:さまくみねしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:福島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:いわき市の建築物]] [[Category:いわき市の歴史]] [[Category:磐城平藩]]

link|Q63147976}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Samakumine Shrine |Image = [[File:Samakumine Jinja Haiden.jpg|270px|]]
Worship Hall |Location = {{jalink|Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707}}{{jalink|Iwaki City|Q274384}}{{jalink|Taira Nakayama|Q128846768}} Aza Miyashita 81 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Isotakeru no Mikoto}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (minor)
Former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}} |Founded = First year of {{jalink|Keiun|Q428753}} ({{jalink|704|Q23503}}) |Main hall = {{jalink|Nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |Alternate name = |Pilgrimage affiliation = |Annual festival = {{jalink|May 4|Q2549}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Samakumine
Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Samakumine Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Samakumine Shrine''' (さまくみねじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707}}, {{jalink|Iwaki City|Q274384}}, {{jalink|Taira Nakayama|Q128846768}}. It is listed as a minor shrine in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} and was formerly ranked as a gōsha under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}. === History === According to shrine tradition, it was founded in the first year of {{jalink|Keiun|Q428753}} ({{jalink|704|Q23503}}). In the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}, it is listed as one of seven shrines in {{jalink|Mutsu Province|Q907389}}, {{jalink|Ishiro District|Q11585428|Iwaki District}}, and was classified as a minor shrine. During the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, it received patronage from the {{jalink|Iwakitaira Domain|Q843782}}. When it was destroyed by lightning in the second year of {{jalink|Tenna (Japan)|Q1145289|Tenna}} ({{jalink|1682|Q7648}}), the shrine buildings were rebuilt the following year by the domain lord {{jalink|Naitō Yoshimune|Q8514975|Naitō Yoshiyasu}}, and thereafter successive lords carried out further repairs and renovations. In the ninth year of {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} ({{jalink|1876|Q7766}}), it was officially designated as a gōsha. === Shrine grounds === File:Samakumine Jinja Honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Samakumine Jinja Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura Hall === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|Q11658590}} === External links === * [http://xn--3iq3y595abz0ateam90u.jp/ Samakumine Shrine] (official website) {{Shinto footer}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Samakumine Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:福島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:いわき市の建築物]] [[Category:いわき市の歴史]] [[Category:磐城平藩]]

Worship Hall |Location = {{jalink|Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707}}{{jalink|Iwaki City|Q274384}}{{jalink|Taira Nakayama|Q128846768}} Aza Miyashita 81 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Isotakeru no Mikoto}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (minor)
Former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}} |Founded = First year of {{jalink|Keiun|Q428753}} ({{jalink|704|Q23503}}) |Main hall = {{jalink|Nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |Alternate name = |Pilgrimage affiliation = |Annual festival = {{jalink|May 4|Q2549}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Samakumine
Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Samakumine Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Samakumine Shrine''' (さまくみねじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707}}, {{jalink|Iwaki City|Q274384}}, {{jalink|Taira Nakayama|Q128846768}}. It is listed as a minor shrine in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} and was formerly ranked as a gōsha under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}. === History === According to shrine tradition, it was founded in the first year of {{jalink|Keiun|Q428753}} ({{jalink|704|Q23503}}). In the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}, it is listed as one of seven shrines in {{jalink|Mutsu Province|Q907389}}, {{jalink|Ishiro District|Q11585428|Iwaki District}}, and was classified as a minor shrine. During the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, it received patronage from the {{jalink|Iwakitaira Domain|Q843782}}. When it was destroyed by lightning in the second year of {{jalink|Tenna (Japan)|Q1145289|Tenna}} ({{jalink|1682|Q7648}}), the shrine buildings were rebuilt the following year by the domain lord {{jalink|Naitō Yoshimune|Q8514975|Naitō Yoshiyasu}}, and thereafter successive lords carried out further repairs and renovations. In the ninth year of {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} ({{jalink|1876|Q7766}}), it was officially designated as a gōsha. === Shrine grounds === File:Samakumine Jinja Honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Samakumine Jinja Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura Hall === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|Q11658590}} === External links === * [http://xn--3iq3y595abz0ateam90u.jp/ Samakumine Shrine] (official website) {{Shinto footer}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Samakumine Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:福島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:いわき市の建築物]] [[Category:いわき市の歴史]] [[Category:磐城平藩]]

link|Q63147976}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Samakumine Shrine |Image = [[File:Samakumine Jinja Haiden.jpg|270px|]]
Worship Hall |Location = {{jalink|Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707}}{{jalink|Iwaki City|Q274384}}{{jalink|Taira Nakayama|Q128846768}} Aza Miyashita 81 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Isotakeru no Mikoto}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (minor)
Former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}} |Founded = First year of {{jalink|Keiun|Q428753}} ({{jalink|704|Q23503}}) |Main hall = {{jalink|Nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |Alternate name = |Pilgrimage affiliation = |Annual festival = {{jalink|May 4|Q2549}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Samakumine
Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Samakumine Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Samakumine Shrine''' (さまくみねじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707}}, {{jalink|Iwaki City|Q274384}}, {{jalink|Taira Nakayama|Q128846768}}. It is listed as a minor shrine in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} and was formerly ranked as a gōsha under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}. === History === According to shrine tradition, it was founded in the first year of {{jalink|Keiun|Q428753}} ({{jalink|704|Q23503}}). In the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}, it is listed as one of seven shrines in {{jalink|Mutsu Province|Q907389}}, {{jalink|Ishiro District|Q11585428|Iwaki District}}, and was classified as a minor shrine. During the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, it received patronage from the {{jalink|Iwakitaira Domain|Q843782}}. When it was destroyed by lightning in the second year of {{jalink|Tenna (Japan)|Q1145289|Tenna}} ({{jalink|1682|Q7648}}), the shrine buildings were rebuilt the following year by the domain lord {{jalink|Naitō Yoshimune|Q8514975|Naitō Yoshiyasu}}, and thereafter successive lords carried out further repairs and renovations. In the ninth year of {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} ({{jalink|1876|Q7766}}), it was officially designated as a gōsha. === Shrine grounds === File:Samakumine Jinja Honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Samakumine Jinja Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura Hall === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|Q11658590}} === External links === * [http://xn--3iq3y595abz0ateam90u.jp/ Samakumine Shrine] (official website) {{Shinto footer}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Samakumine Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:福島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:いわき市の建築物]] [[Category:いわき市の歴史]] [[Category:磐城平藩]]

link|Q63147976}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Samakumine Shrine |Image = [[File:Samakumine Jinja Haiden.jpg|270px|]]
Worship Hall |Location = {{jalink|Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707}}{{jalink|Iwaki City|Q274384}}{{jalink|Taira Nakayama|Q128846768}} Aza Miyashita 81 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Isotakeru no Mikoto}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (minor)
Former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}} |Founded = First year of {{jalink|Keiun|Q428753}} ({{jalink|704|Q23503}}) |Main hall = {{jalink|Nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |Alternate name = |Pilgrimage affiliation = |Annual festival = {{jalink|May 4|Q2549}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Samakumine
Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Samakumine Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Samakumine Shrine''' (さまくみねじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707}}, {{jalink|Iwaki City|Q274384}}, {{jalink|Taira Nakayama|Q128846768}}. It is listed as a minor shrine in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} and was formerly ranked as a gōsha under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}. === History === According to shrine tradition, it was founded in the first year of {{jalink|Keiun|Q428753}} ({{jalink|704|Q23503}}). In the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}, it is listed as one of seven shrines in {{jalink|Mutsu Province|Q907389}}, {{jalink|Ishiro District|Q11585428|Iwaki District}}, and was classified as a minor shrine. During the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, it received patronage from the {{jalink|Iwakitaira Domain|Q843782}}. When it was destroyed by lightning in the second year of {{jalink|Tenna (Japan)|Q1145289|Tenna}} ({{jalink|1682|Q7648}}), the shrine buildings were rebuilt the following year by the domain lord {{jalink|Naitō Yoshimune|Q8514975|Naitō Yoshiyasu}}, and thereafter successive lords carried out further repairs and renovations. In the ninth year of {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} ({{jalink|1876|Q7766}}), it was officially designated as a gōsha. === Shrine grounds === File:Samakumine Jinja Honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Samakumine Jinja Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura Hall === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|Q11658590}} === External links === * [http://xn--3iq3y595abz0ateam90u.jp/ Samakumine Shrine] (official website) {{Shinto footer}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Samakumine Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Samakumine Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:福島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:いわき市の建築物]] [[Category:いわき市の歴史]] [[Category:磐城平藩]]

link|Q63147976}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Samakumine Shrine |Image = [[File:Samakumine Jinja Haiden.jpg|270px|]]
Worship Hall |Location = {{jalink|Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707}}{{jalink|Iwaki City|Q274384}}{{jalink|Taira Nakayama|Q128846768}} Aza Miyashita 81 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Isotakeru no Mikoto}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (minor)
Former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}} |Founded = First year of {{jalink|Keiun|Q428753}} ({{jalink|704|Q23503}}) |Main hall = {{jalink|Nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |Alternate name = |Pilgrimage affiliation = |Annual festival = {{jalink|May 4|Q2549}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Samakumine
Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Samakumine Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Samakumine Shrine''' (さまくみねじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707}}, {{jalink|Iwaki City|Q274384}}, {{jalink|Taira Nakayama|Q128846768}}. It is listed as a minor shrine in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} and was formerly ranked as a gōsha under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}. === History === According to shrine tradition, it was founded in the first year of {{jalink|Keiun|Q428753}} ({{jalink|704|Q23503}}). In the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}, it is listed as one of seven shrines in {{jalink|Mutsu Province|Q907389}}, {{jalink|Ishiro District|Q11585428|Iwaki District}}, and was classified as a minor shrine. During the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, it received patronage from the {{jalink|Iwakitaira Domain|Q843782}}. When it was destroyed by lightning in the second year of {{jalink|Tenna (Japan)|Q1145289|Tenna}} ({{jalink|1682|Q7648}}), the shrine buildings were rebuilt the following year by the domain lord {{jalink|Naitō Yoshimune|Q8514975|Naitō Yoshiyasu}}, and thereafter successive lords carried out further repairs and renovations. In the ninth year of {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} ({{jalink|1876|Q7766}}), it was officially designated as a gōsha. === Shrine grounds === File:Samakumine Jinja Honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Samakumine Jinja Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura Hall === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|Q11658590}} === External links === * [http://xn--3iq3y595abz0ateam90u.jp/ Samakumine Shrine] (official website) {{Shinto footer}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Samakumine Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Samakumine Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Samakumine Shrine]][[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:福島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:いわき市の建築物]] [[Category:いわき市の歴史]] [[Category:磐城平藩]]

link|Q63147976}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Samakumine Shrine |Image = [[File:Samakumine Jinja Haiden.jpg|270px|]]
Worship Hall |Location = {{jalink|Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707}}{{jalink|Iwaki City|Q274384}}{{jalink|Taira Nakayama|Q128846768}} Aza Miyashita 81 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Isotakeru no Mikoto}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (minor)
Former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}} |Founded = First year of {{jalink|Keiun|Q428753}} ({{jalink|704|Q23503}}) |Main hall = {{jalink|Nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |Alternate name = |Pilgrimage affiliation = |Annual festival = {{jalink|May 4|Q2549}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Samakumine
Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Samakumine Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Samakumine Shrine''' (さまくみねじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707}}, {{jalink|Iwaki City|Q274384}}, {{jalink|Taira Nakayama|Q128846768}}. It is listed as a minor shrine in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} and was formerly ranked as a gōsha under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}. === History === According to shrine tradition, it was founded in the first year of {{jalink|Keiun|Q428753}} ({{jalink|704|Q23503}}). In the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}, it is listed as one of seven shrines in {{jalink|Mutsu Province|Q907389}}, {{jalink|Ishiro District|Q11585428|Iwaki District}}, and was classified as a minor shrine. During the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, it received patronage from the {{jalink|Iwakitaira Domain|Q843782}}. When it was destroyed by lightning in the second year of {{jalink|Tenna (Japan)|Q1145289|Tenna}} ({{jalink|1682|Q7648}}), the shrine buildings were rebuilt the following year by the domain lord {{jalink|Naitō Yoshimune|Q8514975|Naitō Yoshiyasu}}, and thereafter successive lords carried out further repairs and renovations. In the ninth year of {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} ({{jalink|1876|Q7766}}), it was officially designated as a gōsha. === Shrine grounds === File:Samakumine Jinja Honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Samakumine Jinja Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura Hall === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|Q11658590}} === External links === * [http://xn--3iq3y595abz0ateam90u.jp/ Samakumine Shrine] (official website) {{Shinto footer}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Samakumine Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Samakumine Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Samakumine Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:福島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:いわき市の建築物]] [[Category:いわき市の歴史]] [[Category:磐城平藩]]

Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Samakumine Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Samakumine Shrine''' (さまくみねじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707}}, {{ill|lt=Iwaki City|Q274384|WD=Q274384}}, {{ill|lt=Taira Nakayama|Q128846768|WD=Q128846768}}. It is listed as a minor shrine in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} and was formerly ranked as a gōsha under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}. === History === According to shrine tradition, it was founded in the first year of {{ill|lt=Keiun|Q428753|WD=Q428753}} ({{ill|lt=704|Q23503|WD=Q23503}}). In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, it is listed as one of seven shrines in {{ill|lt=Mutsu Province|Q907389|WD=Q907389}}, {{ill|lt=Ishiro District|Q11585428|WD=Q11585428|lt=Iwaki District}}, and was classified as a minor shrine. During the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, it received patronage from the {{ill|lt=Iwakitaira Domain|Q843782|WD=Q843782}}. When it was destroyed by lightning in the second year of {{ill|lt=Tenna (Japan)|Q1145289|WD=Q1145289|lt=Tenna}} ({{ill|lt=1682|Q7648|WD=Q7648}}), the shrine buildings were rebuilt the following year by the domain lord {{ill|lt=Naitō Yoshimune|Q8514975|WD=Q8514975|lt=Naitō Yoshiyasu}}, and thereafter successive lords carried out further repairs and renovations. In the ninth year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} ({{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766}}), it was officially designated as a gōsha. === Shrine grounds === File:Samakumine Jinja Honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Samakumine Jinja Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura Hall === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|Q11658590|WD=Q11658590}} === External links === * [http://xn--3iq3y595abz0ateam90u.jp/ Samakumine Shrine] (official website) {{Shinto footer}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Samakumine Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Samakumine Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Samakumine Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:福島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:いわき市の建築物]] [[Category:いわき市の歴史]] [[Category:磐城平藩]]

When it was destroyed by lightning in the second year of {{ill|lt=Tenna (Japan)|Q1145289|WD=Q1145289|lt=Tenna}} ({{ill|lt=1682|Q7648|WD=Q7648}}), the shrine buildings were rebuilt the following year by the domain lord {{ill|lt=Naitō Yoshimune|Q8514975|WD=Q8514975|lt=Naitō Yoshiyasu}}, and thereafter successive lords carried out further repairs and renovations. In the ninth year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} ({{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766}}), it was officially designated as a gōsha. === Shrine grounds === File:Samakumine Jinja Honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Samakumine Jinja Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura Hall === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|Q11658590|WD=Q11658590}} === External links === * [http://xn--3iq3y595abz0ateam90u.jp/ Samakumine Shrine] (official website) {{Shinto footer}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Samakumine Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Samakumine Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Samakumine Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:福島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:いわき市の建築物]] [[Category:いわき市の歴史]] [[Category:磐城平藩]]

Worship Hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707}}{{ill|lt=Iwaki City|Q274384|WD=Q274384}}{{ill|lt=Taira Nakayama|Q128846768|WD=Q128846768}} Aza Miyashita 81 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Isotakeru no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |Founded = First year of {{ill|lt=Keiun|Q428753|WD=Q428753}} ({{ill|lt=704|Q23503|WD=Q23503}}) |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |Alternate name = |Pilgrimage affiliation = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=May 4|Q2549|WD=Q2549}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Samakumine
Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Samakumine Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Samakumine Shrine''' (さまくみねじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707}}, {{ill|lt=Iwaki City|Q274384|WD=Q274384}}, {{ill|lt=Taira Nakayama|Q128846768|WD=Q128846768}}. It is listed as a minor shrine in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} and was formerly ranked as a gōsha under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}. === History === According to shrine tradition, it was founded in the first year of {{ill|lt=Keiun|Q428753|WD=Q428753}} ({{ill|lt=704|Q23503|WD=Q23503}}). In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, it is listed as one of seven shrines in {{ill|lt=Mutsu Province|Q907389|WD=Q907389}}, {{ill|lt=Ishiro District|Q11585428|WD=Q11585428|lt=Iwaki District}}, and was classified as a minor shrine. During the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, it received patronage from the {{ill|lt=Iwakitaira Domain|Q843782|WD=Q843782}}. When it was destroyed by lightning in the second year of {{ill|lt=Tenna (Japan)|Q1145289|WD=Q1145289|lt=Tenna}} ({{ill|lt=1682|Q7648|WD=Q7648}}), the shrine buildings were rebuilt the following year by the domain lord {{ill|lt=Naitō Yoshimune|Q8514975|WD=Q8514975|lt=Naitō Yoshiyasu}}, and thereafter successive lords carried out further repairs and renovations. In the ninth year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} ({{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766}}), it was officially designated as a gōsha. === Shrine grounds === File:Samakumine Jinja Honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Samakumine Jinja Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura Hall === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|Q11658590|WD=Q11658590}} === External links === * [http://xn--3iq3y595abz0ateam90u.jp/ Samakumine Shrine] (official website) {{Shinto footer}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Samakumine Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Samakumine Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Samakumine Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:福島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:いわき市の建築物]] [[Category:いわき市の歴史]] [[Category:磐城平藩]]

Worship Hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707}}{{ill|lt=Iwaki City|Q274384|WD=Q274384}}{{ill|lt=Taira Nakayama|Q128846768|WD=Q128846768}} Aza Miyashita 81 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Isotakeru no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |Founded = First year of {{ill|lt=Keiun|Q428753|WD=Q428753}} ({{ill|lt=704|Q23503|WD=Q23503}}) |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |Alternate name = |Pilgrimage affiliation = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=May 4|Q2549|WD=Q2549}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Samakumine
Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Samakumine Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Samakumine Shrine''' (さまくみねじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707}}, {{ill|lt=Iwaki City|Q274384|WD=Q274384}}, {{ill|lt=Taira Nakayama|Q128846768|WD=Q128846768}}. It is listed as a minor shrine in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} and was formerly ranked as a gōsha under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}. === History === According to shrine tradition, it was founded in the first year of {{ill|lt=Keiun|Q428753|WD=Q428753}} ({{ill|lt=704|Q23503|WD=Q23503}}). In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, it is listed as one of seven shrines in {{ill|lt=Mutsu Province|Q907389|WD=Q907389}}, {{ill|lt=Ishiro District|Q11585428|WD=Q11585428|lt=Iwaki District}}, and was classified as a minor shrine. During the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, it received patronage from the {{ill|lt=Iwakitaira Domain|Q843782|WD=Q843782}}. When it was destroyed by lightning in the second year of {{ill|lt=Tenna (Japan)|Q1145289|WD=Q1145289|lt=Tenna}} ({{ill|lt=1682|Q7648|WD=Q7648}}), the shrine buildings were rebuilt the following year by the domain lord {{ill|lt=Naitō Yoshimune|Q8514975|WD=Q8514975|lt=Naitō Yoshiyasu}}, and thereafter successive lords carried out further repairs and renovations. In the ninth year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} ({{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766}}), it was officially designated as a gōsha. === Shrine grounds === File:Samakumine Jinja Honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Samakumine Jinja Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura Hall === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|Q11658590|WD=Q11658590}} === External links === * [http://xn--3iq3y595abz0ateam90u.jp/ Samakumine Shrine] (official website) {{Shinto footer}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Samakumine Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Samakumine Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Samakumine Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:福島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:いわき市の建築物]] [[Category:いわき市の歴史]] [[Category:磐城平藩]]

link|Q63147976}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Samakumine Shrine |Image = [[File:Samakumine Jinja Haiden.jpg|270px|]]
Worship Hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707}}{{ill|lt=Iwaki City|Q274384|WD=Q274384}}{{ill|lt=Taira Nakayama|Q128846768|WD=Q128846768}} Aza Miyashita 81 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |Founded = First year of {{ill|lt=Keiun|Q428753|WD=Q428753}} ({{ill|lt=704|Q23503|WD=Q23503}}) |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |Alternate name = |Pilgrimage affiliation = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=May 4|Q2549|WD=Q2549}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Samakumine
Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Samakumine Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Samakumine Shrine''' (さまくみねじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707}}, {{ill|lt=Iwaki City|Q274384|WD=Q274384}}, {{ill|lt=Taira Nakayama|Q128846768|WD=Q128846768}}. It is listed as a minor shrine in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} and was formerly ranked as a gōsha under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}. === History === According to shrine tradition, it was founded in the first year of {{ill|lt=Keiun|Q428753|WD=Q428753}} ({{ill|lt=704|Q23503|WD=Q23503}}). In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, it is listed as one of seven shrines in {{ill|lt=Mutsu Province|Q907389|WD=Q907389}}, {{ill|lt=Ishiro District|Q11585428|WD=Q11585428|lt=Iwaki District}}, and was classified as a minor shrine. During the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, it received patronage from the {{ill|lt=Iwakitaira Domain|Q843782|WD=Q843782}}. When it was destroyed by lightning in the second year of {{ill|lt=Tenna (Japan)|Q1145289|WD=Q1145289|lt=Tenna}} ({{ill|lt=1682|Q7648|WD=Q7648}}), the shrine buildings were rebuilt the following year by the domain lord {{ill|lt=Naitō Yoshimune|Q8514975|WD=Q8514975|lt=Naitō Yoshiyasu}}, and thereafter successive lords carried out further repairs and renovations. In the ninth year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} ({{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766}}), it was officially designated as a gōsha. === Shrine grounds === File:Samakumine Jinja Honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Samakumine Jinja Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura Hall === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|Q11658590|WD=Q11658590}} === External links === * [http://xn--3iq3y595abz0ateam90u.jp/ Samakumine Shrine] (official website) {{Shinto footer}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Samakumine Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Samakumine Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Samakumine Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:福島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:いわき市の建築物]] [[Category:いわき市の歴史]] [[Category:磐城平藩]]

name in kana (P1814) == * さまくみねじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 3380005006167 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '福島県いわき市平中山宮下19', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:佐麻久嶺神社]] {{wikidata link|Q63147976}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Samakumine Shrine |Image = [[File:Samakumine Jinja Haiden.jpg|270px|]]
Worship Hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707}}{{ill|lt=Iwaki City|Q274384|WD=Q274384}}{{ill|lt=Taira Nakayama|Q128846768|WD=Q128846768}} Aza Miyashita 81 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |Founded = First year of {{ill|lt=Keiun|Q428753|WD=Q428753}} ({{ill|lt=704|Q23503|WD=Q23503}}) |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |Alternate name = |Pilgrimage affiliation = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=May 4|Q2549|WD=Q2549}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Samakumine
Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Samakumine Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Samakumine Shrine''' (さまくみねじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707}}, {{ill|lt=Iwaki City|Q274384|WD=Q274384}}, {{ill|lt=Taira Nakayama|Q128846768|WD=Q128846768}}. It is listed as a minor shrine in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} and was formerly ranked as a gōsha under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}. === History === According to shrine tradition, it was founded in the first year of {{ill|lt=Keiun|Q428753|WD=Q428753}} ({{ill|lt=704|Q23503|WD=Q23503}}). In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, it is listed as one of seven shrines in {{ill|lt=Mutsu Province|Q907389|WD=Q907389}}, {{ill|lt=Ishiro District|Q11585428|WD=Q11585428|lt=Iwaki District}}, and was classified as a minor shrine. During the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, it received patronage from the {{ill|lt=Iwakitaira Domain|Q843782|WD=Q843782}}. When it was destroyed by lightning in the second year of {{ill|lt=Tenna (Japan)|Q1145289|WD=Q1145289|lt=Tenna}} ({{ill|lt=1682|Q7648|WD=Q7648}}), the shrine buildings were rebuilt the following year by the domain lord {{ill|lt=Naitō Yoshimune|Q8514975|WD=Q8514975|lt=Naitō Yoshiyasu}}, and thereafter successive lords carried out further repairs and renovations. In the ninth year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} ({{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766}}), it was officially designated as a gōsha. === Shrine grounds === File:Samakumine Jinja Honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Samakumine Jinja Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura Hall === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|Q11658590|WD=Q11658590}} === External links === * [http://xn--3iq3y595abz0ateam90u.jp/ Samakumine Shrine] (official website) {{Shinto footer}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Samakumine Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Samakumine Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Samakumine Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:福島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:いわき市の建築物]] [[Category:いわき市の歴史]] [[Category:磐城平藩]]

link|Q63147976}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Samakumine Shrine |Image = [[File:Samakumine Jinja Haiden.jpg|270px|]]
Worship Hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Iwaki City|Q274384|WD=Q274384|1=Iwaki, Fukushima}}{{ill|lt=Taira Nakayama|Q128846768|WD=Q128846768|1=Taira Nakayama}} Aza Miyashita 81 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru no Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Founded = First year of {{ill|lt=Keiun|Q428753|WD=Q428753|1=Keiun}} ({{ill|lt=704|Q23503|WD=Q23503|1=704}}) |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Alternate name = |Pilgrimage affiliation = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=May 4|Q2549|WD=Q2549|1=May 4}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Samakumine
Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Samakumine Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Samakumine Shrine''' (さまくみねじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}, {{ill|lt=Iwaki City|Q274384|WD=Q274384|1=Iwaki, Fukushima}}, {{ill|lt=Taira Nakayama|Q128846768|WD=Q128846768|1=Taira Nakayama}}. It is listed as a minor shrine in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} and was formerly ranked as a gōsha under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === History === According to shrine tradition, it was founded in the first year of {{ill|lt=Keiun|Q428753|WD=Q428753|1=Keiun}} ({{ill|lt=704|Q23503|WD=Q23503|1=704}}). In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as one of seven shrines in {{ill|lt=Mutsu Province|Q907389|WD=Q907389|1=Mutsu Province}}, {{ill|lt=Ishiro District|Q11585428|WD=Q11585428|lt=Iwaki District|1=Iwaki district}}, and was classified as a minor shrine. During the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it received patronage from the {{ill|lt=Iwakitaira Domain|Q843782|WD=Q843782|1=Iwakitaira Domain}}. When it was destroyed by lightning in the second year of {{ill|lt=Tenna (Japan)|Q1145289|WD=Q1145289|lt=Tenna|1=Tenna}} ({{ill|lt=1682|Q7648|WD=Q7648|1=1682}}), the shrine buildings were rebuilt the following year by the domain lord {{ill|lt=Naitō Yoshimune|Q8514975|WD=Q8514975|lt=Naitō Yoshiyasu|1=Naitō Yoshimune}}, and thereafter successive lords carried out further repairs and renovations. In the ninth year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} ({{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766|1=1876}}), it was officially designated as a gōsha. === Shrine grounds === File:Samakumine Jinja Honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Samakumine Jinja Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura Hall === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|Q11658590|WD=Q11658590|1=List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province}} === External links === * [http://xn--3iq3y595abz0ateam90u.jp/ Samakumine Shrine] (official website) {{Shinto footer}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Samakumine Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Samakumine Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Samakumine Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:福島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:いわき市の建築物]] [[Category:いわき市の歴史]] [[Category:磐城平藩]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * さまくみねじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 3380005006167 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '福島県いわき市平中山宮下19', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:佐麻久嶺神社]] {{wikidata link|Q63147976}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Samakumine Shrine |Image = [[File:Samakumine Jinja Haiden.jpg|270px|]]
Worship Hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Iwaki City|Q274384|WD=Q274384|1=Iwaki, Fukushima}}{{ill|lt=Taira Nakayama|Q128846768|WD=Q128846768|1=Taira Nakayama}} Aza Miyashita 81 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru no Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Founded = First year of {{ill|lt=Keiun|Q428753|WD=Q428753|1=Keiun}} ({{ill|lt=704|Q23503|WD=Q23503|1=704}}) |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Alternate name = |Pilgrimage affiliation = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=May 4|Q2549|WD=Q2549|1=May 4}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Samakumine
Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Samakumine Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Samakumine Shrine''' (さまくみねじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}, {{ill|lt=Iwaki City|Q274384|WD=Q274384|1=Iwaki, Fukushima}}, {{ill|lt=Taira Nakayama|Q128846768|WD=Q128846768|1=Taira Nakayama}}. It is listed as a minor shrine in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} and was formerly ranked as a gōsha under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === History === According to shrine tradition, it was founded in the first year of {{ill|lt=Keiun|Q428753|WD=Q428753|1=Keiun}} ({{ill|lt=704|Q23503|WD=Q23503|1=704}}). In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as one of seven shrines in {{ill|lt=Mutsu Province|Q907389|WD=Q907389|1=Mutsu Province}}, {{ill|lt=Ishiro District|Q11585428|WD=Q11585428|lt=Iwaki District|1=Iwaki district}}, and was classified as a minor shrine. During the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it received patronage from the {{ill|lt=Iwakitaira Domain|Q843782|WD=Q843782|1=Iwakitaira Domain}}. When it was destroyed by lightning in the second year of {{ill|lt=Tenna (Japan)|Q1145289|WD=Q1145289|lt=Tenna|1=Tenna}} ({{ill|lt=1682|Q7648|WD=Q7648|1=1682}}), the shrine buildings were rebuilt the following year by the domain lord {{ill|lt=Naitō Yoshimune|Q8514975|WD=Q8514975|lt=Naitō Yoshiyasu|1=Naitō Yoshimune}}, and thereafter successive lords carried out further repairs and renovations. In the ninth year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} ({{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766|1=1876}}), it was officially designated as a gōsha. === Shrine grounds === File:Samakumine Jinja Honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Samakumine Jinja Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura Hall === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|Q11658590|WD=Q11658590|1=List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province}} === External links === * [http://xn--3iq3y595abz0ateam90u.jp/ Samakumine Shrine] (official website) {{Shinto footer}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Samakumine Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Samakumine Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Samakumine Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:福島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:いわき市の建築物]] [[Category:いわき市の歴史]] [[Category:磐城平藩]]

link|Q63147976}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Samakumine Shrine |Image = [[File:Samakumine Jinja Haiden.jpg|270px|]]
Worship Hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Iwaki City|Q274384|WD=Q274384|1=Iwaki, Fukushima}}{{ill|lt=Taira Nakayama|Q128846768|WD=Q128846768|1=Taira Nakayama}} Aza Miyashita 81 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru no Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Founded = First year of {{ill|lt=Keiun|Q428753|WD=Q428753|1=Keiun}} ({{ill|lt=704|Q23503|WD=Q23503|1=704}}) |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Alternate name = |Pilgrimage affiliation = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=May 4|Q2549|WD=Q2549|1=May 4}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Samakumine
Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Samakumine Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Samakumine Shrine''' (さまくみねじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}, {{ill|lt=Iwaki City|Q274384|WD=Q274384|1=Iwaki, Fukushima}}, {{ill|lt=Taira Nakayama|Q128846768|WD=Q128846768|1=Taira Nakayama}}. It is listed as a minor shrine in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} and was formerly ranked as a gōsha under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === History === According to shrine tradition, it was founded in the first year of {{ill|lt=Keiun|Q428753|WD=Q428753|1=Keiun}} ({{ill|lt=704|Q23503|WD=Q23503|1=704}}). In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as one of seven shrines in {{ill|lt=Mutsu Province|Q907389|WD=Q907389|1=Mutsu Province}}, {{ill|lt=Ishiro District|Q11585428|WD=Q11585428|lt=Iwaki District|1=Iwaki district}}, and was classified as a minor shrine. During the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it received patronage from the {{ill|lt=Iwakitaira Domain|Q843782|WD=Q843782|1=Iwakitaira Domain}}. When it was destroyed by lightning in the second year of {{ill|lt=Tenna (Japan)|Q1145289|WD=Q1145289|lt=Tenna|1=Tenna}} ({{ill|lt=1682|Q7648|WD=Q7648|1=1682}}), the shrine buildings were rebuilt the following year by the domain lord {{ill|lt=Naitō Yoshimune|Q8514975|WD=Q8514975|lt=Naitō Yoshiyasu|1=Naitō Yoshimune}}, and thereafter successive lords carried out further repairs and renovations. In the ninth year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} ({{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766|1=1876}}), it was officially designated as a gōsha. === Shrine grounds === File:Samakumine Jinja Honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Samakumine Jinja Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura Hall === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|Q11658590|WD=Q11658590|1=List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province}} === External links === * [http://xn--3iq3y595abz0ateam90u.jp/ Samakumine Shrine] (official website) {{Shinto footer}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Samakumine Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Samakumine Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:福島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:いわき市の建築物]] [[Category:いわき市の歴史]] [[Category:磐城平藩]]

link|Q63147976}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Samakumine Shrine |Image = [[File:Samakumine Jinja Haiden.jpg|270px|]]
Worship Hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Iwaki City|Q274384|WD=Q274384|1=Iwaki, Fukushima}}{{ill|lt=Taira Nakayama|Q128846768|WD=Q128846768|1=Taira Nakayama}} Aza Miyashita 81 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru no Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Founded = First year of {{ill|lt=Keiun|Q428753|WD=Q428753|1=Keiun}} ({{ill|lt=704|Q23503|WD=Q23503|1=704}}) |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Alternate name = |Pilgrimage affiliation = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=May 4|Q2549|WD=Q2549|1=May 4}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Samakumine
Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Samakumine Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Samakumine Shrine''' (さまくみねじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}, {{ill|lt=Iwaki City|Q274384|WD=Q274384|1=Iwaki, Fukushima}}, {{ill|lt=Taira Nakayama|Q128846768|WD=Q128846768|1=Taira Nakayama}}. It is listed as a minor shrine in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} and was formerly ranked as a gōsha under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === History === According to shrine tradition, it was founded in the first year of {{ill|lt=Keiun|Q428753|WD=Q428753|1=Keiun}} ({{ill|lt=704|Q23503|WD=Q23503|1=704}}). In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as one of seven shrines in {{ill|lt=Mutsu Province|Q907389|WD=Q907389|1=Mutsu Province}}, {{ill|lt=Ishiro District|Q11585428|WD=Q11585428|lt=Iwaki District|1=Iwaki district}}, and was classified as a minor shrine. During the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it received patronage from the {{ill|lt=Iwakitaira Domain|Q843782|WD=Q843782|1=Iwakitaira Domain}}. When it was destroyed by lightning in the second year of {{ill|lt=Tenna (Japan)|Q1145289|WD=Q1145289|lt=Tenna|1=Tenna}} ({{ill|lt=1682|Q7648|WD=Q7648|1=1682}}), the shrine buildings were rebuilt the following year by the domain lord {{ill|lt=Naitō Yoshimune|Q8514975|WD=Q8514975|lt=Naitō Yoshiyasu|1=Naitō Yoshimune}}, and thereafter successive lords carried out further repairs and renovations. In the ninth year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} ({{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766|1=1876}}), it was officially designated as a gōsha. === Shrine grounds === File:Samakumine Jinja Honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Samakumine Jinja Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura Hall === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|Q11658590|WD=Q11658590|1=List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province}} === External links === * [http://xn--3iq3y595abz0ateam90u.jp/ Samakumine Shrine] (official website) {{Shinto footer}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Samakumine Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Samakumine Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:福島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:Buildings in Iwaki, Fukushima]] [[Category:いわき市の歴史]] [[Category:磐城平藩]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * さまくみねじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 3380005006167 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '福島県いわき市平中山宮下19', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:佐麻久嶺神社]] {{wikidata link|Q63147976}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Samakumine Shrine |Image = [[File:Samakumine Jinja Haiden.jpg|270px|]]
Worship Hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Iwaki City|Q274384|WD=Q274384|1=Iwaki, Fukushima}}{{ill|lt=Taira Nakayama|Q128846768|WD=Q128846768|1=Taira Nakayama}} Aza Miyashita 81 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru no Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Founded = First year of {{ill|lt=Keiun|Q428753|WD=Q428753|1=Keiun}} ({{ill|lt=704|Q23503|WD=Q23503|1=704}}) |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Alternate name = |Pilgrimage affiliation = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=May 4|Q2549|WD=Q2549|1=May 4}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Samakumine
Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Samakumine Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Samakumine Shrine''' (さまくみねじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}, {{ill|lt=Iwaki City|Q274384|WD=Q274384|1=Iwaki, Fukushima}}, {{ill|lt=Taira Nakayama|Q128846768|WD=Q128846768|1=Taira Nakayama}}. It is listed as a minor shrine in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} and was formerly ranked as a gōsha under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === History === According to shrine tradition, it was founded in the first year of {{ill|lt=Keiun|Q428753|WD=Q428753|1=Keiun}} ({{ill|lt=704|Q23503|WD=Q23503|1=704}}). In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as one of seven shrines in {{ill|lt=Mutsu Province|Q907389|WD=Q907389|1=Mutsu Province}}, {{ill|lt=Ishiro District|Q11585428|WD=Q11585428|lt=Iwaki District|1=Iwaki district}}, and was classified as a minor shrine. During the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it received patronage from the {{ill|lt=Iwakitaira Domain|Q843782|WD=Q843782|1=Iwakitaira Domain}}. When it was destroyed by lightning in the second year of {{ill|lt=Tenna (Japan)|Q1145289|WD=Q1145289|lt=Tenna|1=Tenna}} ({{ill|lt=1682|Q7648|WD=Q7648|1=1682}}), the shrine buildings were rebuilt the following year by the domain lord {{ill|lt=Naitō Yoshimune|Q8514975|WD=Q8514975|lt=Naitō Yoshiyasu|1=Naitō Yoshimune}}, and thereafter successive lords carried out further repairs and renovations. In the ninth year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} ({{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766|1=1876}}), it was officially designated as a gōsha. === Shrine grounds === File:Samakumine Jinja Honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Samakumine Jinja Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura Hall === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|Q11658590|WD=Q11658590|1=List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province}} === External links === * [http://xn--3iq3y595abz0ateam90u.jp/ Samakumine Shrine] (official website) {{Shinto footer}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Samakumine Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Samakumine Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:福島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:Buildings in Iwaki, Fukushima]] [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:磐城平藩]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * さまくみねじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 3380005006167 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '福島県いわき市平中山宮下19', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:佐麻久嶺神社]] {{wikidata link|Q63147976}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Samakumine Shrine |Image = [[File:Samakumine Jinja Haiden.jpg|270px|]]
Worship Hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Iwaki City|Q274384|WD=Q274384|1=Iwaki, Fukushima}}{{ill|lt=Taira Nakayama|Q128846768|WD=Q128846768|1=Taira Nakayama}} Aza Miyashita 81 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru no Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Founded = First year of {{ill|lt=Keiun|Q428753|WD=Q428753|1=Keiun}} ({{ill|lt=704|Q23503|WD=Q23503|1=704}}) |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Alternate name = |Pilgrimage affiliation = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=May 4|Q2549|WD=Q2549|1=May 4}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Samakumine
Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Samakumine Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Samakumine Shrine''' (さまくみねじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}, {{ill|lt=Iwaki City|Q274384|WD=Q274384|1=Iwaki, Fukushima}}, {{ill|lt=Taira Nakayama|Q128846768|WD=Q128846768|1=Taira Nakayama}}. It is listed as a minor shrine in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} and was formerly ranked as a gōsha under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === History === According to shrine tradition, it was founded in the first year of {{ill|lt=Keiun|Q428753|WD=Q428753|1=Keiun}} ({{ill|lt=704|Q23503|WD=Q23503|1=704}}). In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as one of seven shrines in {{ill|lt=Mutsu Province|Q907389|WD=Q907389|1=Mutsu Province}}, {{ill|lt=Ishiro District|Q11585428|WD=Q11585428|lt=Iwaki District|1=Iwaki district}}, and was classified as a minor shrine. During the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it received patronage from the {{ill|lt=Iwakitaira Domain|Q843782|WD=Q843782|1=Iwakitaira Domain}}. When it was destroyed by lightning in the second year of {{ill|lt=Tenna (Japan)|Q1145289|WD=Q1145289|lt=Tenna|1=Tenna}} ({{ill|lt=1682|Q7648|WD=Q7648|1=1682}}), the shrine buildings were rebuilt the following year by the domain lord {{ill|lt=Naitō Yoshimune|Q8514975|WD=Q8514975|lt=Naitō Yoshiyasu|1=Naitō Yoshimune}}, and thereafter successive lords carried out further repairs and renovations. In the ninth year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} ({{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766|1=1876}}), it was officially designated as a gōsha. === Shrine grounds === File:Samakumine Jinja Honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Samakumine Jinja Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura Hall === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|Q11658590|WD=Q11658590|1=List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province}} === External links === * [http://xn--3iq3y595abz0ateam90u.jp/ Samakumine Shrine] (official website) {{Shinto footer}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Samakumine Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Samakumine Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:福島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:Buildings in Iwaki, Fukushima]] [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:磐城平藩]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * さまくみねじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 3380005006167 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '福島県いわき市平中山宮下19', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:佐麻久嶺神社]] {{wikidata link|Q63147976}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Samakumine Shrine |Image = [[File:Samakumine Jinja Haiden.jpg|270px|]]
Worship Hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Iwaki City|Q274384|WD=Q274384|1=Iwaki, Fukushima}}{{ill|lt=Taira Nakayama|Q128846768|WD=Q128846768|1=Taira Nakayama}} Aza Miyashita 81 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru no Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Founded = First year of {{ill|lt=Keiun|Q428753|WD=Q428753|1=Keiun}} ({{ill|lt=704|Q23503|WD=Q23503|1=704}}) |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Alternate name = |Pilgrimage affiliation = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=May 4|Q2549|WD=Q2549|1=May 4}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Samakumine
Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Samakumine Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Samakumine Shrine''' (さまくみねじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}, {{ill|lt=Iwaki City|Q274384|WD=Q274384|1=Iwaki, Fukushima}}, {{ill|lt=Taira Nakayama|Q128846768|WD=Q128846768|1=Taira Nakayama}}. It is listed as a minor shrine in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} and was formerly ranked as a gōsha under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === History === According to shrine tradition, it was founded in the first year of {{ill|lt=Keiun|Q428753|WD=Q428753|1=Keiun}} ({{ill|lt=704|Q23503|WD=Q23503|1=704}}). In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as one of seven shrines in {{ill|lt=Mutsu Province|Q907389|WD=Q907389|1=Mutsu Province}}, {{ill|lt=Ishiro District|Q11585428|WD=Q11585428|lt=Iwaki District|1=Iwaki district}}, and was classified as a minor shrine. During the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it received patronage from the {{ill|lt=Iwakitaira Domain|Q843782|WD=Q843782|1=Iwakitaira Domain}}. When it was destroyed by lightning in the second year of {{ill|lt=Tenna (Japan)|Q1145289|WD=Q1145289|lt=Tenna|1=Tenna}} ({{ill|lt=1682|Q7648|WD=Q7648|1=1682}}), the shrine buildings were rebuilt the following year by the domain lord {{ill|lt=Naitō Yoshimune|Q8514975|WD=Q8514975|lt=Naitō Yoshiyasu|1=Naitō Yoshimune}}, and thereafter successive lords carried out further repairs and renovations. In the ninth year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} ({{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766|1=1876}}), it was officially designated as a gōsha. === Shrine grounds === File:Samakumine Jinja Honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Samakumine Jinja Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura Hall === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|Q11658590|WD=Q11658590|1=List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province}} === External links === * [http://xn--3iq3y595abz0ateam90u.jp/ Samakumine Shrine] (official website) {{Shinto footer}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Samakumine Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Samakumine Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:福島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:Buildings in Iwaki, Fukushima]] [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:磐城平藩]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * さまくみねじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 3380005006167 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '福島県いわき市平中山宮下19', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:佐麻久嶺神社]] {{wikidata link|Q63147976}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Samakumine Shrine |Image = [[File:Samakumine Jinja Haiden.jpg|270px|]]
Worship Hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Iwaki City|Q274384|WD=Q274384|1=Iwaki, Fukushima}}{{ill|lt=Taira Nakayama|Q128846768|WD=Q128846768|1=Taira Nakayama}} Aza Miyashita 81 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru no Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Founded = First year of {{ill|lt=Keiun|Q428753|WD=Q428753|1=Keiun}} ({{ill|lt=704|Q23503|WD=Q23503|1=704}}) |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Alternate name = |Pilgrimage affiliation = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=May 4|Q2549|WD=Q2549|1=May 4}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Samakumine
Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Samakumine Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Samakumine Shrine''' (さまくみねじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}, {{ill|lt=Iwaki City|Q274384|WD=Q274384|1=Iwaki, Fukushima}}, {{ill|lt=Taira Nakayama|Q128846768|WD=Q128846768|1=Taira Nakayama}}. It is listed as a minor shrine in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} and was formerly ranked as a gōsha under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === History === According to shrine tradition, it was founded in the first year of {{ill|lt=Keiun|Q428753|WD=Q428753|1=Keiun}} ({{ill|lt=704|Q23503|WD=Q23503|1=704}}). In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as one of seven shrines in {{ill|lt=Mutsu Province|Q907389|WD=Q907389|1=Mutsu Province}}, {{ill|lt=Ishiro District|Q11585428|WD=Q11585428|lt=Iwaki District|1=Iwaki district}}, and was classified as a minor shrine. During the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it received patronage from the {{ill|lt=Iwakitaira Domain|Q843782|WD=Q843782|1=Iwakitaira Domain}}. When it was destroyed by lightning in the second year of {{ill|lt=Tenna (Japan)|Q1145289|WD=Q1145289|lt=Tenna|1=Tenna}} ({{ill|lt=1682|Q7648|WD=Q7648|1=1682}}), the shrine buildings were rebuilt the following year by the domain lord {{ill|lt=Naitō Yoshimune|Q8514975|WD=Q8514975|lt=Naitō Yoshiyasu|1=Naitō Yoshimune}}, and thereafter successive lords carried out further repairs and renovations. In the ninth year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} ({{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766|1=1876}}), it was officially designated as a gōsha. === Shrine grounds === File:Samakumine Jinja Honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Samakumine Jinja Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura Hall === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|Q11658590|WD=Q11658590|1=List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province}} === External links === * [http://xn--3iq3y595abz0ateam90u.jp/ Samakumine Shrine] (official website) {{Shinto footer}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Samakumine Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Samakumine Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:福島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:Buildings in Iwaki, Fukushima]] [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:磐城平藩]]
