== name in kana (P1814) == * さまくみねじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 3380005006167 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '福島県いわき市平中山宮下19', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:佐麻久嶺神社]] {{wikidata link|Q63147976}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Samakumine Shrine |Image = [[File:Samakumine Jinja Haiden.jpg|270px|]]
Worship Hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Iwaki City|Q274384|WD=Q274384|1=Iwaki, Fukushima}}{{ill|lt=Taira Nakayama|Q128846768|WD=Q128846768|1=Taira Nakayama}} Aza Miyashita 81 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru no Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Founded = First year of {{ill|lt=Keiun|Q428753|WD=Q428753|1=Keiun}} ({{ill|lt=704|Q23503|WD=Q23503|1=704}}) |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Alternate name = |Pilgrimage affiliation = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=May 4|Q2549|WD=Q2549|1=May 4}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Samakumine
Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Samakumine Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Samakumine Shrine''' (さまくみねじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}, {{ill|lt=Iwaki City|Q274384|WD=Q274384|1=Iwaki, Fukushima}}, {{ill|lt=Taira Nakayama|Q128846768|WD=Q128846768|1=Taira Nakayama}}. It is listed as a minor shrine in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} and was formerly ranked as a gōsha under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === History === According to shrine tradition, it was founded in the first year of {{ill|lt=Keiun|Q428753|WD=Q428753|1=Keiun}} ({{ill|lt=704|Q23503|WD=Q23503|1=704}}). In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as one of seven shrines in {{ill|lt=Mutsu Province|Q907389|WD=Q907389|1=Mutsu Province}}, {{ill|lt=Ishiro District|Q11585428|WD=Q11585428|lt=Iwaki District|1=Iwaki district}}, and was classified as a minor shrine. During the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it received patronage from the {{ill|lt=Iwakitaira Domain|Q843782|WD=Q843782|1=Iwakitaira Domain}}. When it was destroyed by lightning in the second year of {{ill|lt=Tenna (Japan)|Q1145289|WD=Q1145289|lt=Tenna|1=Tenna}} ({{ill|lt=1682|Q7648|WD=Q7648|1=1682}}), the shrine buildings were rebuilt the following year by the domain lord {{ill|lt=Naitō Yoshimune|Q8514975|WD=Q8514975|lt=Naitō Yoshiyasu|1=Naitō Yoshimune}}, and thereafter successive lords carried out further repairs and renovations. In the ninth year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} ({{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766|1=1876}}), it was officially designated as a gōsha. === Shrine grounds === File:Samakumine Jinja Honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Samakumine Jinja Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura Hall === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|Q11658590|WD=Q11658590|1=List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province}} === External links === * [http://xn--3iq3y595abz0ateam90u.jp/ Samakumine Shrine] (official website) {{Shinto footer}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Samakumine Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Samakumine Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:福島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Iwaki, Fukushima]] [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:磐城平藩]]

Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Samakumine Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Samakumine Shrine''' (さまくみねじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}, {{ill|lt=Iwaki City|Q274384|WD=Q274384|1=Iwaki, Fukushima}}, {{ill|lt=Taira Nakayama|Q128846768|WD=Q128846768|1=Taira Nakayama}}. It is listed as a minor shrine in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} and was formerly ranked as a gōsha under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === History === According to shrine tradition, it was founded in the first year of {{ill|lt=Keiun|Q428753|WD=Q428753|1=Keiun}} ({{ill|lt=704|Q23503|WD=Q23503|1=704}}). In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as one of seven shrines in {{ill|lt=Mutsu Province|Q907389|WD=Q907389|1=Mutsu Province}}, {{ill|lt=Ishiro District|Q11585428|WD=Q11585428|lt=Iwaki District|1=Iwaki district}}, and was classified as a minor shrine. During the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it received patronage from the {{ill|lt=Iwakitaira Domain|Q843782|WD=Q843782|1=Iwakitaira Domain}}. When it was destroyed by lightning in the second year of {{ill|lt=Tenna (Japan)|Q1145289|WD=Q1145289|lt=Tenna|1=Tenna}} ({{ill|lt=1682|Q7648|WD=Q7648|1=1682}}), the shrine buildings were rebuilt the following year by the domain lord {{ill|lt=Naitō Yoshimune|Q8514975|WD=Q8514975|lt=Naitō Yoshiyasu|1=Naitō Yoshimune}}, and thereafter successive lords carried out further repairs and renovations. In the ninth year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} ({{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766|1=1876}}), it was officially designated as a gōsha. === Shrine grounds === File:Samakumine Jinja Honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Samakumine Jinja Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura Hall === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|Q11658590|WD=Q11658590|1=List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province}} === External links === * [http://xn--3iq3y595abz0ateam90u.jp/ Samakumine Shrine] (official website) {{Shinto footer}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Samakumine Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Samakumine Shrine]][[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:福島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Iwaki, Fukushima]] [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:磐城平藩]]
